title: neurogramma triangularis
parts: Neurogramma triangularis has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, which contain compounds like iridoid glycosides, specifically neurogranin, and the flowers, which contain flavonoids like rutin and quercetin. The leaves of Neurogramma triangularis have been found to contain caffeic acid and its derivatives, which possess anti-inflammatory properties. The stem of the plant contains sesquiterpenes, specifically guaianolides and eudesmanolides, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. However, I couldn't find detailed information on the medicinal constituents of the seeds of Neurogramma triangularis.
